conclusions.statement: 虔诚天命（pietas）：服从神圣命运，服务家国，是罗马精神的本质
citations: Aeneas's devotion to duty (pietas) prevails... 'So great a task it was to found the Roman nation' (Aeneid I.33)

The ghost of his father Anchises describes the Roman mission as 'concerned with government and civilization' and defines Aeneas's duty: 'Rule the people with your sway, spare the conquered, and war down the proud' (Aeneid VI)
conclusions.primary_source: Britannica - Virgil; World History Encyclopedia - Virgil

conclusions.statement: 意大利乡野的热爱：维吉尔终生热爱意大利农村和耕作的农民，这种情感渗透其全部诗作
citations: Virgil was born of peasant stock, and his love of the Italian countryside and of the people who cultivated it colours all his poetry (Britannica)
The Georgics is a superb plea for the restoration of the traditional agricultural life of Italy (Britannica)

conclusions.statement: 罗马的文明使命：罗马被神圣命定去征服世界，然后将文明与法治传播给被征服的民族
citations: Jupiter's prophecy: 'To these I set no bounds in space or time—I have given them rule without end' (Aeneid I.278-279)
Under Augustus, 'Then shall the harsh generations be softened, and wars shall be laid aside' (Aeneid I.291)
conclusions.primary_source: Britannica - Virgil; World History Encyclopedia - Virgil

conclusions.statement: 从伊壁鸠鲁哲学到斯多葛主义的思想演进：早期诗作反映伊壁鸠鲁哲学，后期逐渐转向斯多葛式责任观
citations: One of his teachers was the Epicurean Siro, and the Epicurean philosophy is substantially reflected in his early poetry but gradually gives way to attitudes more akin to Stoicism (Britannica)
The hatred and fear of civil war is powerfully expressed by both Virgil and his contemporary Horace (Britannica)

conclusions.statement: 个人苦难与历史使命的张力：《埃涅阿斯纪》的伟大之处在于将罗马帝国的宏大叙事与个人情感的挫折（特别是狄多女王）并置
citations: The Aeneid is no panegyric; it sets the achievements and aspirations of the giant organization of Roman governmental rule in tension with the frustrated hopes and sufferings of individuals (Britannica)
In Book IV Dido confesses her love for Aeneas, who is forced by the gods to set sail again. Heartbroken by his departure, Dido commits suicide (World History Encyclopedia - The Aeneid)
conclusions.primary_source: Britannica - Virgil; World History Encyclopedia - The Aeneid

conclusions.statement: 对内战的深刻厌恶与对和平的渴望：维吉尔亲历罗马内战，对战争有切肤之痛
citations: Virgil's life was devoted entirely to his poetry... his health was never robust, and he played no part in military or political life (Britannica)
One of the most disastrous effects of the civil wars... was the depopulation of rural Italy (Britannica)

key_concepts.pietas: 虔诚天命：对神明、家族和国家的责任感，是罗马美德的根本
key_concepts.fatum_destinum: 命运：神圣命定，罗马的扩张是天意的安排
key_concepts.dido_tragedy: 狄多之死：个人情感与历史使命的不可调和
key_concepts.golden_age: 黄金时代：奥古斯都统治下的和平与道德复兴
key_concepts.civic_virtues: 公民美德：勇敢、节俭、责任、家庭奉献
